summary: Pass --allowedTools to CLI path so twining-lite tool restriction is enforced
context: Run d18ab582 showed twining-lite and full-twining producing nearly identical results (78.7 vs 77.5, 161 vs 157 Twining calls). The CLI executeTask() path never passed --allowedTools, so twining-lite got all 32 Twining tools instead of the intended 9. The conditions were effectively identical.
rationale: The CLI supports --allowedTools (space-separated tool names). Adding it after --max-turns when agentConfig.allowedTools is defined restores the tool restriction that was lost when switching from SDK to CLI mode. The tool names in twining-lite.ts already use the correct mcp__plugin_twining_twining__* prefix format.

alternatives.option: Use --disallowedTools to block the 23 unwanted tools instead
alternatives.reason_rejected: Fragile — any new Twining tool added in future versions would leak through. Allowlist is safer than blocklist.
--tools controls built-in tools only, not MCP/plugin tools. --allowedTools covers all tool types.
